fix_summary: 1) Per-step lumped energy/branch diagnostics made opt-in (LumpedRuntime.diagnostics_enabled, default off); the field coupling and port V/I observers keep their required tensors (last_voltage_midpoint, last_branch_current). 2) Series auxiliary state (inductor current, capacitor voltage) advanced with an allocation-free in-place kernel schedule that replicates the diagnostic branch's exact elementary op order (separate multiply then add/sub, no fused multiply-add) so it is bitwise-identical. 3) Passive terminations skip the per-step drive waveform evaluation and its allocation.
measurement: Deterministic host/device op tallies from torch.profiler averaged over 16 measured steps after 8 warmup steps. Counts only; NO timing (ms/step) is recorded here per the exclusive-window deferral. 'before' = branch base eb9258b; 'after' = hot-path fix. Harness: scratchpad/profile_hotpath.py.
op_count_contract: tests/rf/lumped/test_fdtd_port_end_to_end.py::test_passive_series_rlc_port_step_stays_within_op_count_ceiling asserts 0 allocations, 0 device-memory, 0 host<->device copies, 0 scalar syncs, <=26 launches/step and <=4 DtoD/step. Falsification: un-gating diagnostics_enabled yields 61 launches, 10 allocs and 15 DtoD per step, turning every ceiling red.
parity: Full 200-step end-to-end port V/I is bitwise-identical to the pre-change base for both a passive resonant SeriesRLC scene (worst-case ULP amplifier) and an active GaussianPulse excitation scene. The existing atol=0 apply-vs-replay adjoint test and the full tests/rf (464) + adjoint (80) suites are green.

bare_fdtd.description: field update block only (one CUDA graph replay budget)

circuit_bound_port.description: apply_port_runtimes + accumulate_port_observers, one circuit-bound port (R-L-C ladder via MNA)

network_bound_port.description: apply_port_runtimes + apply_network_runtimes + accumulate_port_observers, one embedded state-space network port

series_rlc_lumped_port.description: apply_port_runtimes + accumulate_port_observers, one passive SeriesRLC termination

scope_notes: The series SeriesRLC lumped/termination path is the target and shows the full reduction. The embedded network path was already allocation-free (pre-existing GPU observer graph). The circuit-bound (MNA companion) path is a separate module (fdtd/circuits.py), unchanged here; it is now the heaviest per-step port path and is the recommended next target.
title: Port/lumped FDTD per-step hot-path op inventory (before/after)
